zh.lifeSummary: Sir Knight William Perry於1464年出生。他於1487年與Lady Alice Holcombe在英國英格蘭戴文結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1565年4月10日享年101歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Sir Knight William Perry于1464年出生。他于1487年与Lady Alice Holcombe在英国英格兰戴文结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他去世于1565年4月10日享年101岁。

nameMeanings.name: Perry
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Welsh: Anglicized form of Welsh ap Herry ‘son of Herry’, a variant of the personal name Harry (see Harris ).

English: topographic name for someone who lived near a pear tree (Middle English piri(e), peri(e), puri(e), Old English pirige, pyrige), or a habitational name for someone who owned or came from a place so named. This surname and a number of variants have been established in Ireland since the 17th century.

variants.name: Peery
meanings: Irish: variant of   Perry  , taken to Ireland at the time of thePlantation of Ulster and in the Cromwellian period.

variants.name: Pear
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Dov.
English: topographic name from residence by a pear tree (Middle English pere, Old English peru, pere). Compare Perry .
English: nickname from Middle English pere, Old French per, peer ‘peer or paragon’, also ‘companion’.

variants.name: Peary
meanings: English (mainly northern): variant of   Perry  .

History: Robert E. Peary (1856–1920) was a U.S.-born explorer, who in 1909 led the first successful expedition to the North Pole. Peary Land in the north of Greenland is named after him, as he explored there in 1892 and 1900.

variants.name: Perrie
meanings: Scottish: variant of Perry 2. Alternatively, perhaps from the Old French personal name Pierre; see Pierce .

variants.name: Harris
meanings: English (southern England and south Wales): from the personal name Harry + genitival -s. This surname is also established in Ireland, taken there principally during the Plantation of Ulster. However, in some cases, particularly in families coming from County Mayo, Harris can be an Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó hEarchadha. This surname is also very common among African Americans.

American shortened and altered form of Greek surnames begining with Cha(r)-, such as Chasandrinos (variant of Kassandrinos, a habitational name from the Kassandra peninsula of Chalkidiki), and various patronymics from the personal name Charalampos (see Charos ). In North America, the surname Harris may possibly also originate from a transferred use of the Greek personal (given) name Charis or Harris (shortened forms of Charalampos) as a surname (i.e. as a replacement of the original surname).
Americanized form of various like-sounding Jewish surnames.

variants.name: Perryman
meanings: English (Devon): elaborated form of Perry , with Middle English man, probably denoting one who lived near a pear tree; see Pearman .

nameMeanings.name: Sir
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: equivalent of George .

variants.name: George
meanings: Via Old French and Latin, from Greek Georgios (a derivative of geōrgos ‘farmer’, from gē ‘earth’ + ergein ‘to work’). This was the name of several early saints, including the shadowy figure who is now the patron of England (as well as of Germany and Portugal). If the saint existed at all, he was perhaps martyred in Palestine in the persecutions of Christians instigated by the Emperor Diocletian at the beginning of the 4th century. The popular legend in which the hero slays a dragon is a medieval Italian invention. He was for a long time a more important saint in the Orthodox Church than in the West, and the name was not much used in England during the Middle Ages, even after St George came to be regarded as the patron of England in the 14th century. Its use increased from the 1400s, and by 1500 it was regularly among the most popular male names. This popularity was reinforced when George I came to the throne in 1714 , bringing this name with him from Germany. It has been one of the most popular English boys' names ever since.

nameMeanings.name: Knight
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: status or occupational name from Middle English knight ‘retainer, attendant’ (Old English cniht ‘boy, youth, lad)’. The specialized feudal sense ‘a high-ranking tenant bound to serve his lord as a mounted soldier’ is not known to have ever given rise to the surname, although it is not out of the question that it may occasionally have been used as a nickname, perhaps for someone who played the part of an armed knight in a local pageant.
Irish: sometimes adopted for Gaelic Mac an Ridire ‘son of the knight’. See also McKnight .

variants.name: Knights
meanings: English (East Anglia): variant of Knight , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: McKnight
meanings: Irish: part translation of Gaelic Mac an Ridire ‘son of the horseman (Gaelic ridire)’, formerly Anglicized McRuddery.
Scottish (Dumfriesshire and Galloway) and Irish (Ulster): Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Neachtain, which is usually Anglicized as McNaughton .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: Sir Knight William Perry was born in 1464. He married Lady Alice Holcombe in 1487, in Devon, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died on 10 April 1565, at the age of 101.

fr.lifeSummary: Sir Knight William Perry est né en 1464. Il a épousé Lady Alice Holcombe en 1487, à Devon, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé le 10 avril 1565, à l’âge de 101 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Sir Knight William Perry wurde 1464 geboren. Er hat 1487 in Devon, England, United Kingdom Lady Alice Holcombe geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb am 10. April 1565 im Alter von 101 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Sir Knight William Perry è nato il nel 1464. Lui sposato Lady Alice Holcombe nel 1487, nel Devon, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì il 10 aprile 1565, all'età di 101 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Knight William Perryは1464年に生まれた。1487年にイギリスイギリスデボンでLady Alice Holcombeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼は1565年4月10日に101歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Knight William Perry(은)는 1464년 태어났다. 그는 1487년 영국 잉글랜드 데폰에서 Lady Alice Holcombe(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1565년 4월 10일 101세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Sir Knight William Perry nasceu em 1464. Ele casou-se com Lady Alice Holcombe em 1487, em Devon, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu em 10 de abril de 1565, com 101 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Sir Knight William Perry родился в 1464 г. Он женился на женщине по имени Lady Alice Holcombe в 1487 г. в местности девон, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер 10 апреля 1565 г. в возрасте 101 год.

es.lifeSummary: Sir Knight William Perry nació en 1464. Se casó con Lady Alice Holcombe en 1487, en Devon, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió el 10 de abril de 1565, a la edad de 101 años.
